title: Alerts Not Firing — Troubleshooting Guide
content:
When CloudDash alert rules fail to trigger despite conditions appearing met, systematic troubleshooting identifies whether the issue lies in metric ingestion, rule configuration, notification routing, or suppression policies.

Step 1: Verify metric data is flowing. Open the dashboard widget tied to your alert metric and confirm data points appear for the evaluation window. If data is missing, check the cloud provider integration status under Settings > Integrations. A yellow or red status indicator means CloudDash is not receiving metrics. Re-run the connectivity test and verify IAM permissions have not changed.

Step 2: Inspect the alert rule configuration. Navigate to Alerts > Rules, select the affected rule, and review the Evaluation Window, Threshold, and Aggregation settings. CloudDash evaluates rules on a schedule matching your plan: every 1 minute (Enterprise), 2 minutes (Pro), or 5 minutes (Starter). A spike lasting 30 seconds may not trigger a rule with a 5-minute evaluation window.

Step 3: Check alert state and history. The Alert History tab shows evaluation results including 'No Data', 'Threshold Not Met', and 'Suppressed' states. Suppression occurs during maintenance windows (Settings > Maintenance) or when dependency rules block firing.

Step 4: Validate notification channels. Test each channel individually using Alerts > Channels > Send Test Notification. For PagerDuty integration, confirm the routing key matches your PagerDuty service. For Slack, re-authorize the OAuth connection if the bot was removed from the channel.

Step 5: Review rate limiting. CloudDash deduplicates alert notifications using a configurable cooldown period (default 15 minutes). If the same alert fired recently, subsequent triggers are logged but notifications are suppressed until cooldown expires.

If issues persist after these steps, gather the alert rule ID, last 24 hours of evaluation logs, and integration status screenshot before contacting support.
